page.content: The data presented in the accompanying table provides a comprehensive overview of the comparative analysis conducted on two distinct wound closure devices, specifically focusing on their efficacy as measured by the Result (μg/mL) metric. The study included a total of 26 subjects in Group 1, utilizing the absorbable knotless wound closure device, and 36 subjects in Group 2, employing the 2-0 Vicryl closure method. Notably, the variable of age was considered, with mean ages of 70.1 ± 4.8 years for Group 1 and 71.4 ± 8.1 years for Group 2, highlighting the demographic profile of the participants.

included a total of 26 subjects in Group 1, utilizing the absorbable knotless wound closure device, and 36 subjects in Group 2,
employing the 2-0 Vicryl closure method. Notably, the variable of age was considered, with mean ages of 70.1 ± 4.8 years for
Group 1 and 71.4 ± 8.1 years for Group 2, highlighting the demographic profile of the participants.

cells.cell_text: Group 1 absorbable knotless wound closure device (n = 26)

page.content: The statistical significance of the findings is indicated by the p value, which serves as a crucial determinant in evaluating the effectiveness of the respective wound closure techniques. The implications of these results are vital for surgical practices, particularly in optimizing wound healing outcomes among elderly patients, who may present unique challenges in postoperative care. Further analysis may elucidate the impact of age-related factors on wound closure efficacy.
